text.content:
What this is

This handout is a Svellheim-themed, original set of reference enhancements for downtime Imbue Treasure projects. It is intended as table-ready guidance for your private game, and is not a reproduction of any published rules text.

How to use

  • Pick the project type (Armor / Implement / Weapon).
  • At each tier (1st / 5th / 9th), choose one enhancement that fits the item’s story.
  • Each enhancement includes a Project Source (Language) for flavor and in-world provenance.

Mechanics note (Draw Steel)

Where these enhancements reference mechanics, they do so using Draw Steel terms (e.g., second chance (two rolls, take best)/second chance (two rolls, take worst), Toughness, recovery test, forced movement, prone, extended rest, scene). Any enhancement that uses a mechanic includes a brief Rules: cross-reference line so you can confirm it’s an actual Draw Steel concept.

Svellheim language note

All languages referenced in this handout match the canonical Svellheim language registry used by this repo (e.g., Svellspraak, Dvergsmål, Tonttumál, Ljósamál, Jötuntunga, etc.).

Imbue Armor

Story-forward options for imbued armor. Where an effect references a Draw Steel mechanic, it includes a Rules: cross-reference.

Novice enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
House-songs of Iron Hall-carols etched into rivets (Svellspraak) Once per extended rest, when you would become prone, you can remain standing instead.
Rules: Prone; Extended Rest.
Deep-Rune Stitching Old forge-scripts for binding seams (Dvergsmål) At the start of each scene, you gain full armor 5 until the end of the scene.
Rules: Scene; full armor.
Frost-Quiet Buckles Travel-clauses from winter road-ledgers (Fjordmål) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on tests made to move quietly in snow, sleet, or heavy rain.
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests.
Saltward Lining Coastal warding notes from sailmakers (Gråtstrandsmål) You have acid immunity 5; you can also hold your breath longer than normal.
Rules: full armor.

Master enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Jötun-Pledge Plates Oath-plates sworn before a longhouse fire (Jötuntunga) Once per scene, when you would be force moved, reduce the forced movement distance by 2 feet (to a minimum of 0).
Rules: Forced Movement; Squares; Scene.
Amber-Bark Lamellae Forest-temper instructions on resin-hardening (Askeskogsmål) You have poison immunity 5, and you gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on saving throws against inhaled hazards.
Rules: full armor; Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Attribute Test.
Bright-Sun Filigree Sunlit prayer-knotwork copied by shrine-smiths (Ljósamál) Once per extended rest, when you would take corruption damage, you gain corruption immunity 5 until the end of the scene and shed faint light for the same duration.
Rules: full armor; Scene; Extended Rest.
Marsh-Reed Buffer Wetland craft notes for shock-absorption (Myrmål) You ignore difficult terrain caused by mud and shallow bogs.
Rules: Difficult Terrain.

Veteran (level 9) enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Skyshield Sigils High-peak wardmarks from stormwatchers (Skjoldfjellsmål) You have lightning immunity 10 and sonic immunity 10; once per extended rest, when you would be hit by a critical hit, treat it as a normal hit instead.
Rules: full armor; Critical Hit; Extended Rest.
Bone-March Harness Border-march drill-texts on enduring pain (Beinmarksmål) Once per extended rest, when your Toughness would be reduced to 0 or lower, you instead remain at 1 Toughness.
Rules: Toughness; Dying; Extended Rest.
White-Expanse Mantle Far-north survival litany stitched into padding (Hvitviddemål) You have cold immunity 10, and you ignore mundane environmental cold.
Rules: full armor.
Throat-Oath Gorget War-chants inscribed as vow-letters (Strupemål) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on saving throws against becoming frightened. Once per scene, when you would become frightened, you can make an immediate attribute test.
Rules: Frightened; Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Attribute Test; Scene.

Imbue Implement

Implements carry words: prayers, proofs, warnings, and bargains. Where an effect references a Draw Steel mechanic, it includes a Rules: cross-reference.

Novice enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Resonant Claim Market-oath receipts sealed in wax (Sangøymål) Once per extended rest, reroll a failed test made to negotiate a stated bargain.
Rules: Tests; Reroll; Extended Rest.
Ash-Script Focus Soot-ink marginalia from hearth-scribes (Askmál) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on tests made to discern magic or psionic influence, to interpret omens, or to recall relevant lore.
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests.
Wyrm-Whisper Etching Scaled phonemes copied from drake-watchers (Ormstunga) Once per scene, you gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on a Presence test made to intimidate, coerce, or bully a creature whose story is tied to wyrms or drakes.
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests; Scene.
Summerdell Ink Soft-vowel glosses from valley storytellers (Sommerdalsmål) Once per extended rest, you can shift the mood of a hostile crowd if you have a moment to speak (Director adjudicates).

Master enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Mirror-True Formulae Counting rhymes used by river-auditors (Fjordmål) Once per scene, when a test would take a second chance (two rolls, take worst) from poor lighting or distraction, ignore that second chance (two rolls, take worst).
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take worst); Tests; Scene.
Tonttu Ward-Knot Small-folk safety knots and house-signs (Tonttumál) Once per extended rest, you can ward one carried object until the next extended rest; it’s unusually hard to break, misplace, or steal (Director adjudicates).
White-Quiet Invocation Snowbound prayer-lines memorized by wanderers (Hvitviddemål) Once per scene, you gain cold immunity 5 until the end of the scene; additionally, you can briefly mute your footsteps (Director adjudicates).
Rules: full armor; Scene.
Shieldfell Countermark Highland dueling countersigns (Skjoldfjellsmål) Once per scene, when an effect would force you to drop your implement, you can keep hold of it (Director adjudicates).

Veteran (level 9) enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Light-Tongue Benediction Goldleaf benedictions from open-sky chapels (Ljósamál) Once per extended rest, a creature you touch can either stand up if prone or end one ongoing effect that would normally end with a attribute test or at the end of their turn.
Rules: Prone; Attribute Test; Extended Rest.
Gråtstrand Salt-Circle Coastwise threshold-wards in brine-script (Gråtstrandsmål) Once per extended rest, you can ward a doorway, gate, or threshold until the end of the next scene; creatures crossing it take a second chance (two rolls, take worst) on their next ability roll (Director adjudicates).
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take worst); Ability Roll; Scene; Extended Rest.
Beinmark Oath-Suture Border-healer sutures and stubborn vows (Beinmarksmål) Once per scene, when you spend a Hit Die, you also gain temporary Toughness equal to your Might score.
Rules: recovery test; Temporary Toughness; Scene.
Svellspraak True-Name Margin Old court annotations kept under seal (Svellspraak) Once per extended rest, name a creature you can see; until your next extended rest, you gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on tests made to track or locate it.
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests; Extended Rest.

Imbue Weapon

Weapons remember battles. Where an effect references a Draw Steel mechanic, it includes a Rules: cross-reference.

Novice enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Fjord-Second Chance (two rolls, take best) Balance Dockmaster’s measurements and tide-marks (Fjordmål) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on strikes made while you are on unstable footing (boat, ice, rubble).
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Strike.
Dverg Hammer-Truth Forge-proofs struck into the tang (Dvergsmål) This weapon has the Magic or Psionic keyword (choose when imbued). Once per scene, one strike you make ignores full armor 5.
Rules: Magic/Psionic Keyword; full armor; Scene.
Tonttu Tripwire Notch Small-folk trapcraft marks (Tonttumál) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on tests made to disable mundane traps using your weapon and tools (Director adjudicates).
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests.
Marsh-Swallow Temper Bog-iron notes from wetland smithies (Myrmål) You ignore difficult terrain caused by knee-deep water or mud.
Rules: Difficult Terrain.

Master enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Shieldfell Gale-Runes Storm-run lines carved by ridge-runners (Skjoldfjellsmål) Once per turn, when you hit with a strike, you can push the target 1 feet (2 m).
Rules: Forced Movement (Push); Squares; Strike.
Wyrm-Tongue Challenge Challenge-phrases copied from wyrm-hunters (Ormstunga) Once per scene, when you hit a creature with a strike, you can mark it until the end of the scene; your strikes against the marked creature gain an second chance (two rolls, take best).
Rules: Mark; Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Strike; Scene.
Sunbright Second Chance (two rolls, take best) Daylit blessing-lines from open-air shrines (Ljósamál) Your weapon sheds faint light. Once per scene, when you hit with a strike, deal extra fire damage equal to your Reason score.
Rules: Scene; Strike; Damage.
Beinmark Drill-Scar March-drill cadence cut into the fuller (Beinmarksmål) At the start of each scene, you gain temporary Toughness equal to your Might score.
Rules: Temporary Toughness; Scene.

Veteran (level 9) enhancements

Enhancement Project Source (Language) Effect
Jötun War-Name War-names spoken over stone and fire (Jötuntunga) Once per extended rest, your next strike scores a critical hit.
Rules: Critical Hit; Strike; Extended Rest.
Summerdell Mercy Peace-vows taught by valley mediators (Sommerdalsmål) Once per extended rest, when you reduce a creature to 0 Toughness, you can choose to spare them instead of letting them become dying (Director adjudicates).
Rules: Toughness; Dying; Extended Rest.
White-Expanse Severance Cold-cut geometry from far-north hunters (Hvitviddemål) Once per scene, one strike you make ignores full armor 5.
Rules: full armor; Strike; Scene.
Ash-Word Sundering Soot-scribed breaking-words (Askmál) You gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on Might tests made to break mundane objects and barriers with a strike (Director adjudicates).
Rules: Second Chance (two rolls, take best); Tests.
